title: Chapter 39: The Assault
content: The barrier held for eight days.
On the ninth day, it broke.
Roxanne felt it before she heard it — a disturbance in the card, a flicker in its steady pulse. She looked up from her desk just as the alarm system screamed.
"Multiple dimensional rifts," Frost reported, its crystalline fingers moving at impossible speed. "Twelve. No — fifteen. Opening simultaneously around the perimeter."
"How long until they breach?"
"The barrier is already breached. They bypassed it."
"How?"
"They came from below. The 11th Layer doesn't attack from outside. It attacks from underneath."
Roxanne was already running.
The campus was in chaos. Refugees streamed toward the shelters. Children cried. The Executioner stood at the main gate, chainsaw roaring, facing a rift that had opened in the middle of the quad.
From the rift emerged entities Roxanne had never seen before.
They were not Collectors. They were not Frost-bringers. They were older. Darker. Their forms were incomplete — half-formed, shifting, as if they had not fully crossed over from wherever they came.
But they were armed. With weapons that looked like they were made from solidified night.
"11th Layer vanguard," Silas said, appearing beside her. "They're testing our defenses."
"They're terrifying my people."
"That's also part of the test."
She looked at the entities. At the fleeing refugees. At the Executioner standing alone against fifteen rifts.
"How do I stop them?"
"You can't. Not directly. But you can raise the price of staying."
"What does that mean?"
He looked at her.
"Buy the ground they're standing on."
Roxanne didn't question him.

She held up the black card and focused on the ground beneath the entities — the soil of the campus, the stone of the foundations, the dimensional coordinates that anchored Blackwood University to the Nether-Grid.
"I want to buy the territorial rights to every square inch of land currently occupied by 11th Layer entities within the Vance Demesne."
The card pulsed.
A translucent screen appeared in front of her — the same interface she had seen the first time she used the card.

*PURCHASE: TERRITORIAL RECLAMATION — 15 PLOTS
COST: 2,000,000 INFINITY NOTES
CONFIRM?*
"Confirm."
The card flashed.
On the quad, the 11th Layer entities stopped moving.
They looked down at the ground beneath them. Then at each other.
Then they began to sink.
Not into the earth — into a different kind of ground. The ground they no longer had rights to stand on. The territory had been reclaimed. They were trespassing.
One by one, the rifts closed. The entities dissolved into shadow and were pulled back into the 11th Layer.
The quad was silent.
The refugees stared.
Roxanne lowered the card.
"That worked better than I expected."
"You just spent two million Infinity Notes," Frost said. "In one transaction."
"Worth it."
"That was nearly half our reserve."
"Then I'll print more."
Silas looked at her. "The 11th Layer will not try that again. They'll send something that doesn't need to stand on your territory."
"What doesn't need to stand?"
"Something that flies."
Roxanne looked up at the violet sky.
"Then I'll buy the air."
